analysis_result: NF1 mutation frequency in MPNST-G1: 1/8 (12.5%); in MPNST-G2: 5/7 (71.4%). Fisher exact p = 0.0406 (OR=0.057). Paper reported 12.5% vs 71.4%; computed values match the paper.
denominator_used: MPNST-G1 WES cohort: N = 8 samples (all G1 WES==X); MPNST-G2 WES cohort: N = 7 samples (all G2 WES==X). MAF filtered: FILTER=='PASS', Hugo_Symbol=='NF1', non-synonymous (Variant_Classification in Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Splice_Site, Nonstop_Mutation, Translation_Start_Site; Silent excluded).
assumptions_made: Non-synonymous definition follows standard maftools convention (Missense, Nonsense, Frame_Shift_Del/Ins, In_Frame_Del/Ins, Splice_Site, Nonstop_Mutation, Translation_Start_Site); Silent excluded. Barcode mapping: 'Tumor_' + WES label. MPNST_G1 = 8 samples (all with WES==X); MPNST_G2 = 7 samples (one of the 8 G2 samples lacks WES data). All FILTER values in MAF are PASS so PASS filter does not reduce rows. Sample is counted as NF1-mutated if it has at least one non-synonymous PASS NF1 variant.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx (Clinical Data): 108 rows x 15 cols. Used columns: Tumor_ID, Methylation_Group, WES, WES label.
- 41467_2023_38432_MOESM5_ESM.xlsx (Supplementary Table 2 MAF): 7457 rows x 123 cols. Used columns: Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification, FILTER.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- All MPNST_G1 samples with WES==X: N = 8 (barcodes: ['Tumor_M1014', 'Tumor_M2252', 'Tumor_M2346', 'Tumor_M2372', 'Tumor_M3048', 'Tumor_M3398', 'Tumor_M803', 'Tumor_RT6712'])
- All MPNST_G2 samples with WES==X: N = 7 (barcodes: ['Tumor_1019413', 'Tumor_497419', 'Tumor_M1677', 'Tumor_M2325', 'Tumor_M2377', 'Tumor_M2491', 'Tumor_RT1933'])
- G1 barcodes found in MAF: 7/8
- G2 barcodes found in MAF: 7/7
- Note: The paper states G2 has 8 samples but only 7 have WES==X in the clinical data; the paper's 71.4% = 5/7 confirms denominator = 7.

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Tumor_Sample_Barcode = 'Tumor_' + WES label (stringified).
- Non-synonymous Variant_Classification set: {Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Splice_Site, Nonstop_Mutation, Translation_Start_Site}. Silent excluded.
- All MAF rows have FILTER=='PASS'.

D. COMPUTATION
- Filter MAF: FILTER=='PASS' & Hugo_Symbol=='NF1' & Variant_Classification in non-synonymous set. Result: 33 NF1 non-synonymous PASS variants across 24 unique samples.
- G1 samples with NF1 mutation: ['Tumor_M2372'] (n=1).
- G2 samples with NF1 mutation: ['Tumor_1019413', 'Tumor_497419', 'Tumor_M1677', 'Tumor_M2377', 'Tumor_RT1933'] (n=5).
- Fisher exact on 2x2: [[1, 7], [5, 2]].
  scipy.stats.fisher_exact -> OR = 0.0571, p = 0.0406.

E. RESULT
- MPNST-G1: 1/8 = 12.5% with NF1 mutation.
- MPNST-G2: 5/7 = 71.4% with NF1 mutation.
- Fisher exact p = 0.0406, OR = 0.0571.
- Matches paper-reported values of 12.5% (G1) and 71.4% (G2).

F. CAVEATS
- 8 G1 WES samples but only 7 G2 WES samples (one G2 sample lacks WES==X per clinical table). Paper percentages are consistent with 1/8 and 5/7.
- Silent (synonymous) variants are excluded per "non-synonymous" filter; this is the standard maftools convention.
- Sample-level frequencies (any NF1 non-synonymous variant) not variant-level counts.
- 2x2 Fisher exact test with small N; exact p-value reported.
